name: Neighborworks Northeastern Pa/Nhs Of Lackawanna County
description_short: NeighborWorks Northeastern PA is a non-profit housing counselor providing free homebuyer education, home repair assistance, and financial guidance to residents in Lackawanna County.
description_long:
NeighborWorks Northeastern PA operates as a community development non-profit focused on revitalizing neighborhoods and improving housing conditions across northeastern Pennsylvania. The organization has established itself as a local resource for homeowners and prospective buyers seeking quality housing guidance and practical home improvement support. Their mission centers on creating opportunities for individuals and families to improve their lives through quality housing and financial guidance, positioning them within the free-help category of housing and financial counseling services. The organization maintains active community partnerships, including work with the City of Scranton on neighborhood revitalization initiatives.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of free services including homebuyer education workshops, one-on-one homebuyer counseling, and home repair assistance programs. They administer the Beautiful Blocks matching grant program (up to $1,000 per household), provide aging-in-place services specifically for homeowners age 60 and above, and offer wheelchair ramps and home modifications to support seniors. Additionally, they operate a home rehabilitation and resale program that generates funding for their other services, participate in community projects like the Hometown Heroes Banner Program, and produce community-focused content through their Be a Neighbor podcast.

NeighborWorks distinguishes itself through age-specific programming (Aging in Place for seniors 60+), direct home modification services including wheelchair ramps, and integrated community development work that extends beyond individual counseling to neighborhood-level improvements. Their combination of free counseling, matching grants for home improvements, and hands-on home repair assistance through volunteers sets them apart from purely counseling-focused organizations. The organization also demonstrates commitment to hiring locally and maintaining active community engagement through events and media.

The organization appears well-established and mission-driven, though the website provides limited information about counselor credentials or specific HUD certification status. Their services are genuinely free and appear to focus on practical community support rather than financial products. However, prospective clients should verify current program eligibility requirements, availability, and whether specific services have waitlists or geographic restrictions within Lackawanna County.
pros: Free homebuyer education workshops and one-on-one counseling with no apparent fees
Beautiful Blocks matching grant program provides up to $1,000 for home improvements
Aging in Place program specifically serves seniors 60+ with home modifications including wheelchair ramps
Hands-on home repair assistance through volunteer network, not just counseling
Integrated community development approach including neighborhood revitalization partnerships
Home rehabilitation and resale program creates sustainable funding for services
Active community engagement through podcast, events, and local partnerships
cons: Website does not clearly state whether counselors are HUD-certified or specify counselor credentials
No information provided about service availability, wait times, or geographic service area limitations within the region
Limited details about specific topics covered in homebuyer education or eligibility requirements for programs
No mention of debt counseling, credit repair, or financial management services—focused exclusively on housing
Website does not specify hours of operation, response times, or how to schedule one-on-one counseling sessions
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Lackawanna County seeking free education and preparation guidance
Homeowners age 60+ needing aging-in-place modifications and home safety improvements
Residents seeking matching grants for home beautification and exterior improvements up to $1,000
Homeowners in West Scranton and surrounding areas participating in neighborhood revitalization initiatives
diagnosis: NeighborWorks Northeastern PA is best for prospective homebuyers and current homeowners (especially seniors) in Lackawanna County seeking free housing education, home repair assistance, and matching grants for improvements. The main caveat is that this organization does not provide credit counseling, debt management, or financial repair services—they focus exclusively on housing-related support and should be paired with other resources if credit or debt issues are barriers to homeownership.
services: Beautiful Blocks matching grants (up to $1,000 per household)
Aging in Place program for homeowners 60 and above
Wheelchair ramps and home accessibility modifications
Volunteer-based neighborhood cleanup and home repair projects
Hometown Heroes Banner Program for veterans
Be a Neighbor podcast and community content
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
